title: The Sonnet II
content:
Scorn not the Sonnet; Critic, you have frownï¿½d,
Mindless of its just honours; with this key
Shakespeare unlockï¿½d his heart; the melody
Of this small lute gave ease to Petrarchï¿½s wound;
A thousand times this pipe did Tasso sound;
With it Camï¿½ens soothï¿½d an exileï¿½s grief;
The Sonnet glitterï¿½d a gay myrtle leaf
Amid the cypress with which Dante crownï¿½d
His visionary brow: a glow-worm lamp,
It cheerï¿½d mild Spenser, callï¿½d from Faery-land
To struggle through dark ways; and when a damp
Fell round the path of Milton, in his hand
The Thing became a trumpet; whence he blew
Soul-animating strains, alas, too few!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Wordsworth delivers a powerful performance in "The Sonnet II"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
